title: target os mismatch with current os linux - when older runc is in the environment
body:
I think Nalin is looking at this, but I'm not sure.  Also not sure if it's something we can fix in the rpm files or not.  The issue is older versions of runc are causing this error to be raised.  

Workaround: Updating runc on the host machine causes the error to go away.

comments.body: Update runc using --updates-testing and see if the problem goes away.  If it does, give good karma to runc, so we can get it out of updates-testing.

I've run into this today, trying to build an image from inside of a container.  Just for clarity on my part, what version of runc is expected in updates-testing?  My host has runc 1.0.0 (Fedora 26) and the container is 1.0.0-rc3 (CentOS 7.3.1611), with no available updates for either.

IDK if this is the absolute minimum level, but this version and higher should do for you.

runc-1.0.1-1.gitc5ec254.fc26.x86_64

 cat /etc/*release
Fedora release 26 (Twenty Six)
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="26 (Server Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=26
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 26 (Server Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:26"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=26
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=26
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL=https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy
VARIANT="Server Edition"
VARIANT_ID=server
Fedora release 26 (Twenty Six)
Fedora release 26 (Twenty Six)
Fedora 26 should have an update for runc.
@rhatdan Oh good point.  I should have mentioned that I got to that version of runc simply by doing "dnf update runc -y".  I didn't need to retrieve it from source from anywhere.
Keeping this open just so others can find it easily if they encounter the problem on their machines.  I'll close it in a few weeks if Dan/Nalin don't beat me to it.

Looks like my host has the correct version (should have relied on `rpm -v` instead of `runc --version`).

I rebuilt my image with Fedora 26 as it's base image instead of CentOS and got the correct version of runc.  Using buildah inside the image works perfectly with that change.

Thanks!
@lsm5 looks like we need to update the version of buildah and runc on Centos.

@TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind  what's `rpm -q buildah runc` saying for you?

Also, I've changed the koji tags (and thus the yum repo URL) for Virt SIG packages for CentOS. Please make sure to use the yum repo mentioned in https://wiki.centos.org/Cloud/Docker
This is fixed in the current release.
